stories.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
stories.simple_summary: Some AI safety companies are making it harder for cybersecurity researchers to test their tools because they're worried about misuse. The researchers say this makes it tougher to find bugs and protect computers.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech security professionals — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is making Alexa (the voice assistant) smarter so it can control more types of smart home devices like door locks and vacuum cleaners.
stories.why_it_matters: If you have smart home devices, Alexa will soon work with more of them without you having to set things up manually.

stories.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
stories.simple_summary: AMD (a computer chip company) is launching a new powerful computer system to compete with Nvidia, another chip maker.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and large tech companies — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic's Claude AI voice assistant is getting better at understanding complicated spoken requests, like rescheduling meetings or writing emails.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Claude, it will soon be easier to give it voice commands for everyday tasks instead of typing.

stories.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
stories.simple_summary: Claude's voice mode is now available on more powerful versions of the AI and can work with apps like Gmail and Slack.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Claude through these apps, you can now speak to it instead of typing in more situations.

stories.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
stories.simple_summary: A new company called AegisAI is using AI to catch fake emails and phishing scams by spotting unusual patterns.
stories.why_it_matters: Businesses should watch this — it could help protect your company email from criminals trying to trick employees into revealing passwords or money.

stories.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
stories.simple_summary: Patreon (a platform for creators to get paid by fans) is laying off about 20 percent of its workers, partly because AI has changed how the company operates.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows companies using AI to work more efficiently — it doesn't affect most small business owners unless you use Patreon.

stories.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
stories.simple_summary: A company called Runway created a tool that automatically picks the best AI system to create images, videos, or audio depending on what you need.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to professional video and graphic designers — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI's ChatGPT now has a health tool that all US users can try, letting you share your Apple Health data and fitness information with it.
stories.why_it_matters: If you want health advice from an AI, you can now connect your fitness trackers to ChatGPT — but remember it's not a real doctor.

stories.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI is rolling out ChatGPT Health widely and claims its AI is now as good as doctors at understanding medical information.
stories.why_it_matters: Be cautious — an AI tool is helpful information but should never replace talking to your actual doctor about serious health problems.

stories.title: Meta launched a new AI optimism ad set to a song about human extinction
stories.simple_summary: Meta ran an upbeat advertisement about AI using a famous song that's actually about the world ending in five years.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't affect you — it's just an awkward choice of music by Meta for their ad.

stories.title: Nvidia is sending GPUs to the moon
stories.simple_summary: Nvidia is sending its computer chips to the moon for a space project.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to space engineers — nothing you need to act on.
